eco_baseline_footprint.formula: BIF = (vCPU x 15 EUR) + (Storage GB x 0.08 EUR)
eco_baseline_footprint.method_note: RAM is considered included in compute cost and is not monetized separately in the operationalized framework model. The selected baseline is a framework normalization assumption, not the official mainnet validator requirement.
eco_baseline_footprint.source: Framework cost model applied to IOTA comparative MCC

ps_throughput.estimation_note: Official protocol-level throughput claim for Mysticeti-based IOTA. This is not a benchmark measured on the comparative MCC (1 validator + DAG replica, 4 vCPU, 200 GB), but a technology-level classification value derived from the native protocol architecture.
ps_throughput.source: IOTA Technical and Tokenomics Whitepaper (30 May 2025)

eco_variable_transaction_cost.estimation_note: Approximate value derived from the FAQ average fee indication of 0.005 IOTA per average transaction.

eco_storage_footprint.source: Framework baseline storage assumption for IOTA MCC

parallel_execution.motivation: Object-based execution and the owned-object fast path enable parallel processing of independent transactions.
parallel_execution.source: IOTA object model and transaction lifecycle

horizontal_partitioning.motivation: The object model structurally partitions state across owned/shared objects rather than relying on a globally shared account state for every operation.

absence_of_global_serialization.motivation: Owned-object transactions can bypass full global ordering on the fast path; global sequencing remains for checkpoint formation.
absence_of_global_serialization.source: IOTA transaction lifecycle and consensus documentation

scalable_coordination_model.motivation: Mysticeti is documented as high-throughput, leaderless, DAG-oriented BFT coordination.

append_only_structural_history.motivation: Committed transactions form a DAG-based append-only history with checkpoints.

cryptographic_linkage_between_records.motivation: Objects and transactions are cryptographically linked.

cryptographic_write_authentication.motivation: Transactions are signed and certified through quorum validator signatures.

independent_integrity_verification.motivation: Certificates, signatures and finalized checkpoints can be independently verified.

rci_tamper_evidence_auditability.source: IOTA object model, transaction lifecycle and validator committee

protocol_enforced_irreversible_commit.motivation: Commit is based on certificates signed by a quorum and finalized through checkpoints.

multi_party_confirmation_requirement.motivation: A quorum greater than two-thirds of voting power is required.

absence_of_unilateral_rollback_capabilit.motivation: No single validator can unilaterally revoke a certified or finalized transaction.

rci_finality_rollback_resistance.source: IOTA validator committee and transaction lifecycle documentation

rci_write_availability_fault_tolerance.configuration_note: Technology-level score based on the real validator committee model, not on the normalized comparative MCC.

absence_of_single_writer_requirement.motivation: Write requests can be handled by multiple validators in the committee.

quorum_based_write_continuation.motivation: The protocol continues as long as quorum conditions remain satisfied.

automatic_write_continuation_mechanism.motivation: No single validator is required for system-wide write continuation.

multi_node_write_survivability.motivation: The committee model provides survivability against single-node failure when quorum remains available.

rci_write_availability_fault_tolerance.source: IOTA validator committee and consensus model

absence_of_single_entity_unilateral_stat.motivation: State validation and commit depend on the validator committee, not on a single operator.

structural_requirement_for_multi_entity_.motivation: Transactions require quorum signatures from multiple validators.

validator_admission_independence.motivation: Validator participation is tied to delegated stake thresholds and protocol rules rather than a fixed enterprise permissioning authority.

absence_of_structurally_fixed_validator_.motivation: The validator set is epoch-based and can change at reconfiguration boundaries.

ags_control_distribution_governance.source: IOTA governance and validator committee model

native_privilege_differentiation_capabil.motivation: The object ownership model and cryptographic keys do not constitute a native enterprise IAM privilege framework.

native_identity_lifecycle_governance_cap.motivation: IOTA does not provide enterprise-style native identity lifecycle governance comparable to permissioned DLT membership services.

structural_independence_of_identity_gove.motivation: The protocol does not provide a native identity governance framework in the sense required by this criterion.

ags_identity_access_governance.source: IOTA object model and permissionless network model

non_unilateral_compromise_possible.motivation: A single validator cannot unilaterally compromise transaction integrity.

majority_compromise_required.motivation: Compromising safety requires control of a majority of validator voting power.

supermajority_compromise_required.motivation: The BFT quorum threshold is greater than two-thirds of total voting power.

ags_collusion_resilience.source: IOTA validator committee and BFT quorum model
